description: Database design, querying, and optimization knowledge

details.consistency: Database moves from valid state to valid state

forms.2NF: 1NF + No partial dependencies on composite keys
forms.BCNF: 3NF + Every determinant is a candidate key

types.b_tree: Balanced tree for range queries and equality
types.hash: O(1) lookup for equality queries only
types.gin: Generalized inverted index for full-text search
types.gist: Generalized search tree for spatial data

best_practices.name: Query Optimization
guidelines: Use EXPLAIN to understand query plans
Index columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY
Use covering indexes for frequently used queries

best_practices.name: Schema Design
guidelines: Choose appropriate data types (not too large)
Use foreign keys for referential integrity

pagination.cursor_based: SELECT * FROM table WHERE id > :last_id LIMIT 10
pagination.keyset: More efficient than OFFSET for large datasets

aggregation.group_by: SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM items GROUP BY category
aggregation.having: Use HAVING for filtering grouped results

joins.inner: Returns matching rows from both tables
joins.left: All rows from left, matching from right
joins.right: All rows from right, matching from left

pitfalls.name: N+1 Query Problem
pitfalls.description: Making N additional queries instead of one JOIN
pitfalls.example: Fetching user then separately fetching each user's posts

pitfalls.name: Missing Indexes
pitfalls.description: Full table scans on large tables
pitfalls.solution: Add appropriate indexes based on query patterns

pitfalls.name: Unbounded Queries
pitfalls.description: Queries that can return unlimited results
pitfalls.solution: Always use LIMIT, even for 'expected' small results

pitfalls.name: Lock Contention
pitfalls.description: Too many transactions competing for locks
pitfalls.solution: Keep transactions short, use appropriate isolation levels

relational.use_when: Structured data with relationships, ACID needed
